title: Parmigiana di Melanzane Eggplant

seo.title: Parmigiana di Melanzane: Authentic Italian Eggplant Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Italian cooking with our authentic Parmigiana di Melanzane recipe. Layers of tender eggplant, rich tomato sauce, and melted cheese create a vegetarian delight. Perfect for any meal!
longTailKeywords: how to make Parmigiana di Melanzane
Parmigiana di Melanzane step by step
best Parmigiana di Melanzane at home
seo.h1: Authentic Parmigiana di Melanzane: Classic Italian Eggplant Bake
h2Headings: Tips for the Best Parmigiana di Melanzane
seo.imageAlt: A freshly baked dish of authentic Parmigiana di Melanzane, a vegetarian Italian eggplant casserole.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, comforting flavors of Italy with this traditional Parmigiana di Melanzane, a perfect vegetarian classic.

description: Parmigiana di Melanzane is a beloved Italian vegetarian dish, featuring layers of tender fried eggplant, rich homemade tomato sauce, fragrant basil, and generous amounts of mozzarella and Parmesan cheese. Baked to golden perfection, it offers a comforting symphony of flavors and textures, making it an irresistible classic for any occasion. This traditional recipe brings the authentic taste of Italy right to your kitchen.

instructions.title: Prepare the Eggplant
instructions.text: Slice the eggplants into 1/4 inch thick rounds. Lay them on a baking sheet, sprinkle generously with salt, and let them rest for 30 minutes to draw out excess moisture. Pat them thoroughly dry with paper towels before proceeding.

instructions.title: Fry the Eggplant Slices
instructions.text: Heat about 1/4 inch of olive oil in a large pan over medium-high heat. Fry the eggplant slices in batches until golden brown and tender on both sides. Transfer them to a plate lined with paper towels to drain any excess oil. For a lighter version, you can grill or bake the eggplant instead.

instructions.title: Make the Tomato Sauce
instructions.text: In a large pot, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened, about 5 minutes. Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant. Pour in the crushed tomatoes, add salt and pepper to taste, and a few fresh basil leaves. Simmer the sauce for at least 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it thickens slightly.

instructions.title: Assemble the Parmigiana Base
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 190°C (375°F). Spread a thin layer of the prepared tomato sauce on the bottom of a 9x13 inch baking dish. Arrange a single layer of the fried eggplant slices over the sauce, ensuring they overlap slightly if necessary. Top this layer with a portion of the mozzarella slices, a sprinkle of grated Parmigiano Reggiano, and a few fresh basil leaves.

instructions.title: Layer and Repeat
instructions.text: Continue the layering process: add another layer of tomato sauce, followed by eggplant slices, then mozzarella, Parmigiano, and basil. Repeat these layers until all ingredients are used, typically creating 3-4 layers. Ensure you finish with a generous top layer of tomato sauce, mozzarella, and Parmigiano Reggiano.

instructions.title: Bake to Perfection
instructions.text: Place the assembled Parmigiana di Melanzane in the preheated oven and bake for 30-35 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and bubbly. If the cheese starts to brown too quickly, you can loosely cover the dish with aluminum foil for the remaining baking time.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, remove the Parmigiana from the oven and let it rest for at least 10-15 minutes before slicing and serving. This crucial resting period allows the layers to set properly, making for cleaner portions and enhancing the overall texture and flavor. Garnish with additional fresh basil if desired.

tips: Salting the eggplant before frying is a classic Italian technique that removes bitterness and excess water, preventing your Parmigiana from becoming watery.
For a lighter version without sacrificing flavor, grill or bake your eggplant slices instead of frying. Brush them lightly with olive oil before cooking until tender and slightly browned.
Allowing the Parmigiana to rest for at least 10-15 minutes after baking is key. This helps the layers set, making it easier to slice and enhancing the overall texture and depth of flavor.
article: Parmigiana di Melanzane Eggplant is a beloved Italian Vegetarian dish enjoyed across Italian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
